objectives: Deep performance analysis of MX20022 runtime with file:line references across 11 focus areas
important_facts: No [profile.release] section in any Cargo.toml -- no LTO, no codegen-units tuning
RocksDB store does full table scan for every query() and find_by_key_field()
DuplicateChecker performs up to 3 sequential DB queries per message (N+1 pattern)
raw_message (full XML payload) cloned 3+ times per transaction on hot path
TCP outbound opens new connection per send -- no connection pooling
escape_xml chains 5 .replace() allocations on every call
Context stores raw_message as owned String cloned into ContextMeta
update_transaction does read-modify-write (2 round trips) in both Postgres and SQLite
Store trait default count_dead_letters loads all rows then counts
Store trait default count_pending_expectations loads all rows then counts
Store trait default count_transactions_by_states loops N states with full query per state
Metrics use with_label_values on every call -- no caching of label lookups
Correlation engine holds write lock during timeout_scan iteration
No backpressure signal from pipeline to channel beyond mpsc buffer of 1024
risks_issues: RocksDB full scan: will degrade linearly with data growth
No release profile: production binary not optimized
No benchmarks: no regression detection for hot paths

iteration.summary: Comprehensive performance audit covering all 11 focus areas with concrete file:line references. Found 7 Critical Bottleneck, 8 Significant Issue, and 6 Minor Optimization findings plus 3 Good Practice acknowledgments. Top concerns: RocksDB full table scans, excessive raw_message cloning on hot path, N+1 DB queries in DuplicateChecker, no release profile optimization, no benchmarks, TCP connection-per-send, and read-modify-write pattern in update_transaction.
next_actions: Implement fixes if requested, starting with Critical items
